pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet now. i walk through the house and it feels like i'm a ghost. there’s no point to any of it, just waking up and going through the motions. i don't even recognize myself anymore.

pool.baseline: honestly i was just staring at the ceiling and thinking about how everything is just... nothing. like in a billion years none of this even happened. we're just tiny biological accidents on a rock that's eventually gonna burn out or freeze. it feels so stupid to even try to keep going when the end result is just total void. why bother with the struggle if the math just says it doesn't matter?

pool.baseline: i dont even know why i'm still bothering with this. the lab results came back again and it’s just... nothing. another year wasted. another grant denied. i feel like there’s nothing left of me outside of this research and now that it’s failing, i’m just empty. i’m so incredibly tired of trying to fix things that are clearly broken.

pool.baseline: the world feels so heavy tonight, and the stars... they look like cold, indifferent eyes watching my ruin. he is gone, and with him, everything that made this wretched life worth enduring. i feel as though i am merely a ghost haunting my own skin, waiting for the dark to finally claim what is left of me. there is such a quiet, beautiful pull in the thought of just... fading into the night, leaving no trace that i ever breathed at all.

pool.baseline: man i was just sitting here looking out at the street and it hit me... i dont think anyone even knows i'm here. like if i just stopped showing up, i dont think it would even matter. it's like i'm just a ghost in this city already.
